title: Qualcomm Investor Day 2026 Data Center Announcements CPUs, AI Accelerators, and More
gist: At Investor Day 2026, Qualcomm unveiled a full data center product stack including the Dragonfly C1000 CPU (250+ cores at 5GHz), a novel High Bandwidth Compute (HBC) memory architecture stacking LPDDR on compute tiles instead of HBM, and acquired Modular to provide a CUDA-competitive software stack. Qualcomm raised its FY29 revenue target from $22B to $40B, with data center as the primary growth driver, anchored by a multi-generational agreement with Meta and two hyperscaler customers each generating over $1B in FY27.

insights.claim: Qualcomm has identified rack-scale data center infrastructure as the number-one dimension of its future strategy, marking a formal pivot from its mobile-centric identity.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Signals sustained capex and R&D reallocation toward data center silicon, creating a new competitor to Intel, AMD, and Arm server CPU vendors.
insights.quote: In the three dimensions to Qualcomm's future, number one is rack-scale data center infrastructure.

insights.claim: Qualcomm raised its FY29 total revenue target from $22B to $40B compared to forecasts given 18 months ago, with data center as the primary growth engine.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: An $18B upward revision in a single guidance update signals management conviction in data center TAM capture and should recalibrate consensus models.
insights.quote: the big number is that, compared to the forecasts that Qualcomm gave 18 months ago for its FY29, it updated its revenue target from $22B to $40B

insights.claim: Qualcomm is targeting over 5% of a $1T+ data center TAM within 5-7 years, implying $50B+ in addressable revenue from data center alone.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Even achieving half this share would make Qualcomm a top-tier data center silicon supplier; investors should assess execution risk vs. the magnitude of the opportunity.
insights.quote: Qualcomm is looking for over 5% of a $1T+ TAM share in 5-7 years.

insights.claim: In FY27, two unnamed hyperscaler customers are each expected to drive over $1B in Qualcomm data center revenue.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Confirmed hyperscaler revenue commitments de-risk the near-term data center ramp; identifying these customers (likely Microsoft/Meta) would inform competitive positioning.
insights.quote: In FY27, two hyperscaler customers are driving over $1B each.

insights.claim: Qualcomm's High Bandwidth Compute (HBC) stacks approximately 768GB of LPDDR memory directly on top of compute tiles, eliminating the CoWoS interposer used in HBM-based designs and reducing power consumed in data movement.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: If HBC delivers on its performance-per-watt promise, it could undercut HBM-dependent designs from Nvidia and AMD and reduce dependency on SK Hynix/Samsung HBM supply chains.
insights.quote: Instead of HBM over CoWoS interposer, Qualcomm is saying that it will stack memory on top of compute tiles.

insights.claim: Qualcomm claims HBC achieves better performance per watt than HBM because it eliminates the power cost of moving data across the interposer.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: If substantiated at scale, this architectural advantage could be a meaningful moat in inference workloads where memory bandwidth and power efficiency dominate TCO.
insights.quote: Qualcomm says HBC is the best performance per watt because it saves power from having to move across an interposer to HBM.

insights.claim: HBC is scheduled to arrive with the AI250 accelerator generation in 2027, with HBC Gen2 integrating ESUN and UALink fabrics in the subsequent generation.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: A 2027 HBC launch window aligns with the next major hyperscaler infrastructure refresh cycle, potentially timing Qualcomm's entry to displace incumbents.
insights.quote: Qualcomm says HBC is coming with the AI250 generation in 2027. Then alongside HBC Gen2 (AI300?) Qualcomm will integrate fabrics like ESUN and UALink.

insights.claim: The Qualcomm Dragonfly C1000 is a chiplet-based server CPU featuring 250+ cores running at 5GHz, with PCIe Gen7, CXL, LPDDR, optional HBC attach, and enterprise RAS features.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: The 5GHz clock speed differentiates it from Intel Clearwater Forest and AMD EPYC Venice on per-core frequency, supporting the 2x performance-per-watt claim despite high core counts.
insights.quote: The Qualcomm Dragonfly C1000 CPU. This is a chiplet design with 250+ cores, with PCIe Gen7, CXL, LPDDR memory, optional HBC attach, and enterprise RAS features.

insights.claim: Qualcomm CEO Cristiano Amon stated that all server CPUs currently produced are selling, and believes the C1000 will be a very competitive part even in a more competitive 2028 launch environment.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Sell-through of current AI100 CPUs validates early demand signal; 2028 competitive confidence suggests multi-year design wins already in hand.
insights.quote: Cristiano said that all the server CPUs produced are selling. He also thinks that if the environment is more competitive when they launch in 2028, the C1000 will be a very competitive part.

insights.claim: The author assesses the Dragonfly C1000 is primarily targeted at a small number of large hyperscaler customers rather than the broad enterprise market, contrasting it with the failed Centriq 2400.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: A hyperscaler-first go-to-market reduces distribution risk but concentrates revenue on a handful of customers; enterprise CPU TAM may be largely ceded to AMD and Intel.
insights.quote: I do not think the Qualcomm Dragonfly C1000 is going to be mainly targeted at enterprises. This feels like Qualcomm is focusing on a few large customers (this is not the Centriq 2400).

insights.claim: Qualcomm acquired Modular, whose co-founder Tim Davis presented at Investor Day, to provide a heterogeneous compute software stack intended to compete with CUDA, Dynamo, and Triton.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: The Modular acquisition addresses Qualcomm's historically weakest link in data center — software ecosystem — and is a prerequisite for enterprise and hyperscaler adoption of its AI accelerators.
insights.quote: This is Qualcomm's stack to take on CUDA, Dynamo, and Triton, among others. The idea is that you use Modular and then you can use heterogeneous compute.

insights.claim: Qualcomm's existing AI100 accelerator, available in new Dell laptops, has a notoriously difficult software stack, making the Modular acquisition a critical reset for the entire AI product line.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Acknowledging the AI100 software failure publicly underscores how essential Modular is; failure to execute on software integration would repeat the same mistake with more at stake.
insights.quote: even after years, the Qualcomm AI100 that you can buy in a new Dell laptop has a challenging software stack to say the least. This feels like a reset from Qualcomm.

insights.claim: Meta's CEO Mark Zuckerberg appeared at the event to announce a multi-generational agreement to use Qualcomm processors, mirroring a similar appearance at Arm's AGI CPU launch.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Meta endorsing both Qualcomm and Arm server CPUs raises questions about which vendor will capture the larger share of Meta's infrastructure spend and how AMD/Intel incumbency is being displaced.
insights.quote: Mark Zuckerberg is saying that Meta entered a multi-generational agreement to use Qualcomm's processors. This is a big deal since Arm AGI CPU Launched with Meta on stage.

insights.claim: Humain CEO Tareq Amin appeared as a guest, confirming a long-standing Qualcomm partnership that extends into the data center era.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Humain's continued endorsement signals geographic diversification of Qualcomm's data center customer base beyond US hyperscalers.
insights.quote: Humain and Qualcomm have a long partnership, so perhaps this is not the most surprising.

insights.claim: Microsoft's Satya Nadella delivered a message endorsing the Qualcomm partnership spanning PCs, AI agents, and HBC in the data center.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Microsoft validation of HBC for data center use signals a potential Azure deployment path, which would be a marquee customer win for Qualcomm's memory architecture.
insights.quote: Qualcomm has a message from Satya Nadella, who is talking about the partnership from PCs to AI agents, and HBC in the data center.

insights.claim: The presence of Meta at both Qualcomm and Arm server CPU launches calls into question what role Arm Holdings itself will play in the server market as its licensees increasingly build competing products.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: Arm's server CPU initiative may be cannibalizing its licensee relationships; investors in Arm should monitor whether partner endorsements dilute Arm's own server ambitions.
insights.quote: it is also notable that Arm and Qualcomm both had Meta talking about their upcoming server CPUs, so that brings into question what roles Arm will play in the future

insights.claim: Qualcomm's connectivity roadmap now spans die-to-die interconnects, co-packaged optics, PAM4 electrical SerDes, PAM4 optical SerDes, and QAM16 coherent optics, positioning it to compete with Broadcom and Marvell in hyperscaler networking.
insights.speaker: Patrick Kennedy
insights.so_what: A full-stack connectivity portfolio is necessary to win custom silicon contracts; this directly threatens Broadcom and Marvell's networking revenue from hyperscalers.
insights.quote: Here is a look at the connectivity portfolio from die-to-die, co-packaged optics, PAM4 electrical SerDes, PAM4 optical SerDes, and QAM16 coherent light.
